item.title: UAE to Leave OPEC and OPEC+ in May to Pursue New Strategy
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has decided to leave the OPEC and OPEC+ oil producer groups as it plans a strategic realignment in the wake of the Iran war.

item.title: Novogratz’s Galaxy Digital Posts Loss as Crypto Rout Lingers
item.description: Galaxy Digital Inc., the crypto financial services conglomerate founded and run by billionaire Michael Novogratz, posted a narrower-than-estimated first-quarter loss, citing a shift toward data centers in the business mix as recurring fee revenue and transaction income continue to improve. Revenue was higher than analysts’ estimates.

item.title: Can China Really Block Meta’s Manus AI Acquisition?
item.description: Artificial intelligence “agents” that can carry out complex tasks with minimal human intervention are attracting heavy investment as giant technology companies look beyond chatbots to develop more autonomous systems.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Undershoots Forecast on Eve of Rate Decision
item.description: Brazil’s inflation picked up less than expected in early April, driven by higher food and fuel costs stemming from the war in Iran.

item.title: Iran Faces Economic Reckoning as Oil Storage Tanks Fill Up
item.description: Sooner or later, the US blockade may force Iran to cut oil production.

item.title: Steelmaker Cliffs Taps Palantir Technologies for AI Overhaul
item.description: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. struck a three-year agreement with Palantir Technologies Inc. to deploy artificial intelligence tools across its operations, as the US steelmaker steps up efforts to modernize its manufacturing footprint.

item.title: Warren Presses Bank Regulators on Trump’s Call to Cap Card Rates
item.description: Senator Elizabeth Warren pressed US regulators on whether they plan to take action to rein in lenders after President Donald Trump’s push earlier this year for a temporary 10% cap on credit card rates resulted in little change.

item.title: Romania Finance Chief Urges Continued Reforms to Avert Junk Risk
item.description: Romania’s finance minister warned that the current political turmoil is increasing funding costs and may raise the risk of the country losing its investment grade credit score.

item.title: BMG to Buy Concord in Deal That Creates a New Major Music Giant
item.description: German record label BMG Rights Management agreed to buy Concord, a Nashville-based independent music group, in a deal that will create one of the largest music companies in the world.

item.title: Hungary’s Central Bank Holds Key Rate After Post-Election Rally
item.description: Hungary’s central bank kept interest rates unchanged after a post-election market rally, as policymakers await government plans to tackle a bloated budget and gain access to frozen European Union funds.

item.title: Sherwin-Williams Sales Jump Most Since 2023 on Forex Tailwinds
item.description: Sherwin-Williams Co. sales rose the most in three years, as strong industrial demand and favorable exchange rates offset soft DIY demand in North America.

item.title: Russia Ships the Most Crude in Over a Month as Port Attacks Ease
item.description: Ukraine’s drone strikes switch back to refineries, allowing Moscow to boost seaborne flows

item.title: Poland Sees Rising Cyberattacks With Spread of Advanced AI Tools
item.description: Poland, which is battling a rising number of cyberattacks from Russia, expects online threats to intensify as more advanced artificial intelligence tools spread, according to a senior government official.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 28, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.6% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York on concerns about artificial intelligence spending.

item.title: US Stock Futures Slip as Report on OpenAI Sales Miss Drags Tech
item.description: US stock futures declined on Tuesday, as a report of OpenAI missing its internal sales goals fueled worries about artificial intelligence spending and dragged down technology stocks related to the AI startup.

item.title: Spain Sticks With 2026 Growth Forecast Despite Iran War Fallout
item.description: The Spanish government stuck with its growth forecast, with the country’s stronger-than-expected performance in recent years outweighing uncertainty stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: ‘Melt-Up’ Risk Builds as Narrow Tech Rally Propels Stock Market
item.description: Technology megacaps are pushing benchmark indexes to new records while the rest of the market is lagging behind. Traders can be forgiven for feeling like they’ve seen this movie before.

item.title: Foxconn Industrial Falls Short of Lofty AI Expectations
item.description: Foxconn Industrial Internet Co. benefited less than expected from China’s artificial intelligence boom as sales and earnings growth fell short of lofty expectations.

item.title: Gabon Begins Audit of Public Loans to Establish Debt-Stock Total
item.description: Gabon began auditing its public debt to establish which obligations should be formally recognised, as the central African nation seeks a new International Monetary Fund program.

item.title: Sinopec’s First-Quarter Profit Rises Amid Higher Oil Prices
item.description: Sinopec’s profit climbed in the first quarter amid higher crude prices due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Emerging Stocks Slip From Highs as Tech Selloff, Oil Surge Weigh
item.description: Emerging stocks fell on Tuesday, retreating from record highs, as a broad selloff in tech shares and higher oil prices dragged on sentiment.

item.title: Avia Bonds Fall Into Distress as Iran War Roils Travel Industry
item.description: Avia Solutions Group’s bonds have fallen into distressed territory as turmoil in the travel industry caused by the Middle East war dampens demand for the aviation company’s debt.

item.title: VC Firm Hires First Chief AI Officer, Seeking Smarter Use of the Technology
item.description: Eclipse wants to help its portfolio companies get smarter with AI

item.title: Bank of Japan Delivers Hawkish Hold But Yen Remains on Brink
item.description: BOJ Governor Ueda treads carefully as the yen hovers near a level that’s prompted past intervention.

item.title: Global Goods Trade Set to Slow This Year to Half 2025’s Pace
item.description: The oil price shock and protectionism will act as headwinds for cross-border commerce

item.title: Bank of Italy Urges Meloni Government to Keep Eye on Spending
item.description: Italy needs to watch its spending if it wants to stick to European Union fiscal rules despite a worsening economic growth scenario, Bank of Italy official Andrea Brandolini said.

item.title: Barrick Picks New York for Gold Spinoff Later This Year
item.description: Barrick Mining Corp. said it plans to list its North American operations in New York and reaffirmed that it’s on track to complete the initial public offering by the end of this year.

item.title: Danish Jobs Drop, Snapping Growth Streak as Novo Cuts Hit Data
item.description: Denmark’s employed workforce shrank for the first time since mid-2024, as layoffs at Novo Nordisk A/S began to show up in official data.

item.title: US Treasuries Trade Rangebound as Fed Officials Prepare to Meet
item.description: Treasuries fluctuated in a range as Federal Reserve officials prepared to meet in what’s expected to be Jerome Powell’s final meeting as head of the central bank.

item.title: Tech’s ‘New Normal’ Trade Pair: Long Chip Stock, Short Software
item.description: In a choppy year for tech investors, one trade has stood out as a success: buy chip stocks, sell software shares. And the divide between winners and losers is getting bigger as 2026 moves along.
